mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and supports corporate profit margins, triggering immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and industrial production captures aggregate demand-supply equilibrium. When housing starts exceed 2000 thousand units while industrial production remains above 105, consumer and business confidence align to drive positive equity momentum over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term's predictive power vanishes (p > 0.10) when controlling for FEDFUNDS, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-high and low-low proxy quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive 3-month FEDFUNDS increases exceeding 0.25 percentage points per month.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates above 2.5 percent, or the signal produces losses in more than 60% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index greater than 0.8 percent indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which reduces input cost inflation and supports positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in over 50% of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.8 percent, indicating the supply signal fails to translate into equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and increasing retail sales captures synchronized labor and consumer demand strength. This joint expansion validates earnings sustainability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation, or forward 60-day returns show no significant difference between high-payroll/high-sales and low-payroll/low-sales periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle housing expansion where housing starts accelerate while CPI remains below 3.0 percent, speculative demand and stable inflation jointly compress equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when HOUST growth exceeds 10 percent year-over-year while CPIAUCSL simultaneously rises above 3.5 percent, indicating inflation fears override housing demand.
